title: Build Infinite Scrolling
description: Create an infinite scroll feed that auto-loads paginated content as the user scrolls — the pattern behind Twitter, Instagram, and LinkedIn feeds.
requirements: Paginated API or mock data with page parameter
Intersection Observer on a sentinel element at list bottom
Auto-load next page when sentinel enters viewport
Append new items to existing list — do not replace
Loading indicator while fetching next page
End-of-list message when no more pages
Retry button on fetch failure without losing loaded items
useEffect cleanup disconnects IntersectionObserver on unmount
TypeScript types for observer callback entries
Stable keys on list items — no duplicates after append
acceptanceCriteria: Scrolling to bottom triggers exactly one fetch for next page
Page 1 and page 2 items both visible after scroll load
No duplicate items in the list
End message appears when API returns empty or last page
Retry re-fetches failed page without clearing existing items
Observer disconnected on component unmount — no console warnings
npm run build passes with strict TypeScript
hints: Use a ref on a div at the bottom of the list as the observer target.
Track page, hasMore, isLoading, and error in separate state variables.
Guard fetch: if (isLoading || !hasMore) return before loading next page.
Append: setItems(prev => [...prev, ...newItems]) — immutable update preview for Day 12.
TMDB, JSONPlaceholder, or mock paginated API work well.

bonus.title: Feed polish
requirements: Skeleton rows while loading first page
Lazy load images with loading="lazy" attribute

debuggingScenarios.title: Duplicate API calls
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Same page fetched twice; duplicate items in list
debuggingScenarios.cause: Missing isLoading guard or observer firing multiple times before state updates
debuggingScenarios.fix: Guard with isLoading && !hasMore; set isLoading true synchronously before fetch

debuggingScenarios.title: Infinite rendering
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Browser freezes; console shows repeated renders
debuggingScenarios.cause: setState in render body or effect with unstable deps causing loop
debuggingScenarios.fix: Move fetch to observer callback or guarded effect; stable dependency array

debuggingScenarios.title: Observer cleanup
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Memory leak warnings; fetch continues after navigate away
debuggingScenarios.cause: IntersectionObserver not disconnected in useEffect cleanup
